bullets.text: Q3 CR of 89.5 (87.3 YTD); Personal Lines CR 89.5 for Q3, including a 4.6-point impact from $950M Florida excess profit statute accrual; personal property CR an exceptional 63.5 in Q3.
Q3 NPW grew 10% YOY; YTD Personal Lines NPW exceeded $55 billion, up 16% YOY; personal auto NPW grew 13% in Q3 driven by strong renewals.

Companywide PIF growth of 12% YOY; Personal Lines added 750,000+ PIFs in Q3 alone; personal auto PIFs up 15% YOY — sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit PIF growth; 4.1 million new Personal Lines applications processed in Q3, the highest quarter of the year.

Florida excess profit statute accrual of $950M (13.9 CR points in September) reflects 2023 insurance reform delivering lower-than-permitted loss costs; selective rate decreases initiated where margins allow; personal property rates increased ~2% in Q3 (8% YTD).
Auto shopping remained elevated with consistent conversion rates indicating competitive rates; personal property benefited from mild weather and favorable prior-year development.
Financial leverage at 16.3% at Q3 end; no bond maturities until 2027; Q3 investment portfolio returned 1.7% (fixed income 1.5%, equity 8.1%); 6.1% total portfolio return YTD through Q3.

Personal auto product model 8.9 live in 26 states (50% of PIFs); model 9.0 in pilot with four additional state elevations planned; Snapshot 5.0 deployed in 42 states (78% of NPW), adding accident detection and emergency towing features.
Commercial Lines model 8.3 elevated in 10 states (43% of commercial auto NPW); personal property 5.0+ models in 35 states (80%+ of homeowners NPW) with expanded peril rating.
Progressive ranked in the 99th percentile of Gallup-surveyed companies; employee quotes highlighted culture of inclusivity, collaboration, and long-term commitment.
Commercial Lines Q3 NPW decreased 6% primarily due to lower TNC premiums from mileage forecast changes; core commercial auto continued strong PIF growth in business auto and contractor segments.
